summary: Iteration 4 verified end-to-end. Static analysis of /app/rf-bridge/hackrf_scanner.py and mavlink_bridge.py confirms the two fixes for the user's Kali/WSL bugs are correctly in place. Backend regression suite ran 29/29 PASS (21 original + 8 new for /health, /emergency/abort, /report/mission.pdf and dual-registered WS). Frontend E2E on the preview URL completed all 9 army-demo flows without functional regression: login, RESTRICTED banner, dashboard stat tiles, SystemHealth tile (Backend/MongoDB UP, HackRF/SiK DOWN as expected in sandbox), 2-click EMERGENCY ABORT, mavlink broadcast → live pkt appended via WS (rows 17→18), SafetyGate modal opens for deploy-target-PL-005 and cancels cleanly, Mission Log EXPORT PDF triggers a download, logout returns to /login. The user's actual Kali WSL bug is 100% a stale docker backend image; nothing to fix backend-side here.

issues: React hydration warning: <span> cannot be a child of <option>. The <option> template uses `{d.callsign} · {d.model} · sys={d.system_id}` which is plain text so the warning most likely comes from a whitespace-preserving parent style/link elsewhere in the tree. Non-blocking; browser still renders and value is selectable.

Sonner ACCESS GRANTED toast (~4s duration) visually overlaps the EMERGENCY ABORT button. Interaction still works via force click but a real operator's first click may miss. Consider z-index tweak or shrinking the toast width.

static_analysis.rf-bridge/hackrf_scanner.py: PASS — kill_stale_hackrf() defined at line 80, invoked at line 98 (top of check_hackrf) and at line 141 (inside run_sweep_once busy-recovery block). 300ms sleep after kill (line 99) and 500ms sleep after successful hackrf_info (line 116) release the USB handle before hackrf_sweep opens it. Error strings 'Resource busy' and '-1000' are both matched (line 139).
static_analysis.rf-bridge/mavlink_bridge.py: PASS — on_error callback at line 103 inspects `emsg` for '404' (line 106) and logs the exact `docker compose build --no-cache backend && docker compose up -d backend` recovery command (lines 107-113). Token still stripped from URL log (line 75-76).
static_analysis.backend/server.py: PASS — WS route /api/ws/mavlink dual-decorated (@app.websocket line 533 + @api.websocket line 534). /api/health, /api/emergency/abort, /api/report/mission.pdf all mounted on `api` router and require Depends(get_current_user).

backend_smoke./api/health: PASS — shape validated; backend=true, mongo=true, hackrf=false, sik_radio=false, ws_clients int, active_targets int, total_packets_tx int, server_time iso.
backend_smoke./api/emergency/abort: PASS — returns {ok:true, ts:<iso>}; ABORT log entry appears in /api/logs; unauth call returns 401.
backend_smoke./api/report/mission.pdf: PASS — Content-Type application/pdf, Content-Disposition attachment, body begins with %PDF-, size > 5 KB; unauth call returns 401.
backend_smoke.ws_without_token: PASS — handshake rejected (close by server / no receive).

frontend_e2e.system_health_dashboard_tile: PASS (4 rows visible, Backend/MongoDB UP, HackRF/SiK DOWN as expected — no hardware in sandbox)
frontend_e2e.mavlink_broadcast_appends_pkt_via_ws: PASS (17→18 pkt rows after broadcast-btn click)
frontend_e2e.safety_gate_pl005: PASS (modal opens on deploy-target-PL-005; cancel dismisses it cleanly)

action_items: OPTIONAL (design polish): shorten ACCESS GRANTED toast duration or move it to top-center so it doesn't overlap the EmergencyAbort button in the top-right of the header.

OPTIONAL (react warning): investigate hydration warning on Payloads target-select — a <span> is being rendered inside <option>. Options can only contain text. Likely coming from some emoji/span component or from wrapping text with a Trans component. Non-blocking.

USER-FACING (from user's original bug, not fixable here): they must run `docker compose build --no-cache backend && docker compose up -d backend` on their Kali host to pick up the new server.py containing /api/ws/mavlink. rf-bridge/mavlink_bridge.py now surfaces this hint on WS 404 error automatically.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 891 lines — nearing the 700-line split threshold. Consider splitting routers per resource (auth, detections, mavlink, payloads, logs, ws, reports) for maintainability. Not urgent.
The Mission PDF route imports its heavy reportlab deps inside the function (line 713-722) — good pattern to avoid startup cost, keep it.
SafetyGate.jsx correctly enforces 5 checkboxes + double click before firing; visible pulse-crit animation on CONFIRM is a nice UX affordance.
EmergencyAbort auto-clears the 'CONFIRM ABORT' state after 4s (useEffect timer). Good.
seed_data_creation: ~4 simulated detections + 1 MAVLink broadcast + 1 payload PL-005 SafetyGate flow (cancelled, no fire) + 1 EMERGENCY ABORT log entry + 1 PDF report generated during frontend E2E.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py + /app/backend/tests/test_new_endpoints.py for regression. Login credentials operator@cema.mil / cema@2026. When testing the top-right EmergencyAbort button in Playwright, wait ~4-5s after login for the sonner ACCESS GRANTED toast to dismiss, otherwise the first click can be intercepted by the toast. Payloads target-select requires >=1 ACTIVE detection; simulate one via POST /api/detections/simulate first if the dropdown is empty. WS URL for MAVLink is /api/ws/mavlink (dual-registered).
rca of the issue: User's Kali/WSL bugs are 100% caused by their local docker backend container running an older server.py. Backend rebuild = complete fix. Iteration 4 defensive fixes (hackrf zombie kill + WS 404 hint) mitigate the operator experience but do not change the sandbox behaviour, which was already correct.
